name: infinite-gratitude
description: Multi-agent research skill for parallel research execution (10 agents, battle-tested with real case studies).
capabilities: Orchestrates 10 AI agents to conduct parallel research on complex topics
Synthesizes findings from multiple agents into comprehensive summaries
Handles large-scale research tasks that benefit from distributed investigation
Processes information faster than single-agent sequential research
Integrates with Claude Code and Codex for research workflows
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 8 static findings are false positives. External command references are documentation examples (git clone in markdown). Network URLs are GitHub repository links in metadata and documentation. No executable code present. The skill is safe documentation for an external research tool.
valueStatement: Deep research requires comprehensive information from multiple sources. This skill orchestrates 10 AI agents to work in parallel, gathering and synthesizing research faster than sequential methods.
userTitle: Accelerate Research with 10 Parallel AI Agents
limitations: Requires access to the external infinite-gratitude repository for full implementation
Research quality depends on the complexity and availability of source information
Parallel agent execution may consume significant API tokens or resources
Not suitable for simple queries that single-agent processing can handle efficiently

useCases.title: Academic Literature Review
useCases.description: Graduate students use parallel agents to survey research papers across multiple journals and synthesize findings into comprehensive literature reviews.

useCases.title: Competitive Intelligence Gathering
useCases.description: Business analysts deploy multiple agents to research competitors, market trends, and industry developments simultaneously for strategic planning.

useCases.title: Technical Investigation
useCases.description: Engineers investigating complex technical issues can research documentation, forums, and codebases in parallel to understand root causes.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Research Query
promptTemplates.prompt: Research the current state of quantum computing applications in pharmaceutical drug discovery. Use parallel agents to cover different aspects: algorithms, case studies, challenges, and future prospects.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new research topic with broad coverage needs

promptTemplates.title: Comparative Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Compare and contrast three leading cloud providers' serverless computing offerings. Assign agents to research pricing, performance limits, ecosystem support, and use case fit.
promptTemplates.scenario: Evaluating multiple options against specific criteria

promptTemplates.title: Deep Dive Investigation
promptTemplates.prompt: Investigate the root causes of the recent log4j vulnerability. Have agents research the technical details, affected systems, mitigation strategies, detection methods, and long-term security implications.
promptTemplates.scenario: Comprehensive investigation of a specific event or topic

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Perspective Synthesis
promptTemplates.prompt: Research the debate around AI regulation from multiple viewpoints: technical feasibility, economic impact, ethical considerations, policy frameworks, and international comparisons. Synthesize balanced insights.
promptTemplates.scenario: Understanding complex topics with diverse stakeholder perspectives

bestPractices: Define clear research objectives before deploying parallel agents to ensure focused and relevant results
Allow sufficient time for all agents to complete their research streams before synthesis
Review individual agent outputs alongside the synthesized summary for comprehensive understanding
Structure complex research topics into distinct sub-topics for optimal parallel agent assignment
antiPatterns: Using parallel research for simple fact-checking that a single query can resolve efficiently
Deploying all 10 agents on overly narrow topics that result in redundant or duplicate findings
Expecting real-time results - parallel research requires time for thorough investigation and synthesis
Relying solely on synthesized summaries without reviewing individual agent research streams

faq.question: What makes parallel research better than single-agent research?
faq.answer: Parallel research uses multiple agents simultaneously to investigate different aspects of a topic. This provides broader coverage, faster results for complex topics, and diverse perspectives that a single sequential approach might miss.

faq.question: Do I need technical skills to use this skill?
faq.answer: Basic familiarity with AI assistants is helpful. You simply need to clearly define your research question. The skill handles the complexity of orchestrating multiple agents and synthesizing their findings.

faq.question: How long does parallel research take?
faq.answer: Time varies based on research complexity. Simple queries may take a few minutes, while comprehensive investigations can take 15-30 minutes. Parallel processing is faster overall than sequential research, but thorough research requires time.

faq.question: Can I use this for real-time or current events research?
faq.answer: The skill works best for well-established topics with substantial documentation. For breaking news or rapidly evolving situations, supplement with current sources and verify recency of findings.

faq.question: What types of research are best suited for this approach?
faq.answer: Topics that benefit from multiple perspectives, technical deep-dives, comparative analysis, literature reviews, and comprehensive investigations work well. Simple factual queries are better handled by single-agent approaches.

faq.question: How do I get access to the full implementation?
faq.answer: Refer to the official repository at github.com/sstklen/infinite-gratitude for installation instructions and complete documentation on using this multi-agent research system.
